'''Phoenix Laulu-Togaga'e''' (born 16 April 2003) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays at {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Catalans Dragons]] in the Super League, and for [[Keighley Cougars]] in [[RFL League One|League One]]. He has spent time on loan or dual registration at [[Dewsbury Rams]], Keighley and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]], and at [[Rochdale Hornets]] and Oldham in League One.

==Background==
Laulu-Togaga'e was born in [[Brisbane]], Australia and moved to the United Kingdom when he was seven.<ref name="TRL191221">{{cite news |last1=Davidson |first1=John |title=Hull KR's Phoenix Laulu-Togagae sets sights on Super League breakthrough |url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-krs-phoenix-laulu-togagae-sets-sights-on-super-league-breakthrough/ |access-date=26 February 2022 |work=TotalRL.com |date=19 December 2021}}</ref> He is the son of Samoan international rugby league footballer [[Quentin Laulu-Togaga'e]]. At school, Laulu-Togaga'e played [[rugby union]] and was member of the academy side at [[Leicester Tigers]].

==Playing career==
===Keighley Cougars===
With the Leicester Tigers academy side suspended due to the COVID-19 pandemic, Laulu-Togaga'e was invited to train with [[Keighley Cougars]], for whom his father Quentin played. Keighley were impressed with Phoenix and, in March 2021, the club signed him on a one-year contract. After playing in a pre-season game, Laulu-Togaga'e made his professional debut for Keighley against [[West Wales Raiders]] on 13 June 2021.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Messenger |first1=James |title=League 1 round-up: Six wins from six for Barrow, father-son duo combine for Keighley & tries galore for Hunslet and Doncaster |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/league-1-round-up-six-wins-from-six-for-barrow-father-son-duo-combine-for-keighley-tries-galore-for-hunslet-and-doncaster/ |access-date=26 February 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=13 June 2021}}</ref> His father played in the same match and, when Phoenix came on as an [[Interchange (rugby league)|interchange player]], they became the first instance of father and son playing in the same professional match since 2013 and only the third father-son pairing ever.{{efn|The last father-son pairing were [[Brad Hepi]] and [[Tyla Hepi]] for [[Gloucestershire All Golds]], while the only other recorded instance was in 1924 when [[Tedda Courtney|Ed "Tedda" Courtney]] and his son [[Ed Courtney Jr.|Ed Jr.]] played for [[Western Suburbs Magpies]].<ref name="Ruck">{{cite web |title=This Leicester Tigers youngster just played professional Rugby alongside his Dad! |url=https://www.ruck.co.uk/this-leicester-tigers-youngster-just-played-professional-rugby-alongside-his-dad/ |website=Ruck |access-date=26 February 2022 |date=18 March 2021}}</ref>  A fourth father-son pairing occurred in 2022 when [[George Flanagan]] and George Jr appeared for [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Like father, like son for Bulls' Flanagans |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/20288939.george-flanagan-hails-sons-bradford-bulls-debut/ |access-date=24 July 2022 |work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |date=20 July 2022}}</ref>}}<ref>{{cite news |last1=Heppenstall |first1=Ross |title=Like father like son as family play in same Keighley Cougars team and score |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/father-and-son-keighley-cougars-20811737 |access-date=26 February 2022 |work=YorkshireLive |date=14 June 2021}}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
After playing a handful of games for Keighley, Laulu-Togaga'e was spotted by [[Super League]] side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and was signed on a two-year contract in August 2021 but was loaned back to Keighley for the remainder of the [[2021 RFL League 1|2021 season]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Hull KR snap up Phoenix Laulu-Togaga'e |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-snap-up-phoenix-laulu-togagae/ |access-date=26 February 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=11 August 2021}}</ref>

At the end of the 2021 season, Laulu-Togaga'e had made 12 appearances for Keighley, scoring five tries, and won the [[Rugby Football League]]'s League 1 Young Player of the Year award.<ref name="yearbook2021"/><ref>{{cite news |last1=O'Brien |first1=James |title=Lachlan Coote shares first impressions of Phoenix Laulu-Togaga'e |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/lachlan-coote-phoenix-laulutogagae-impressions-6338728 |work=HullLive |date=13 December 2021}}</ref>

Laulu-Togaga'e made his Super League debut for Hull Kingston Rovers on 25 February 2022 as an interchange player during Rovers' 26–10 victory over [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Hull KR 26-10 Castleford: Robins claim first win of 2022 – talking points |url=https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/hull-kr-26-10-castleford-214700047.html |access-date=26 February 2022 |work=uk.sports.yahoo.com |date=25 February 2022}}</ref>

==== Dewsbury Rams (loan) ====
The following week, he appeared for [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Dewsbury Rams]] with whom Hull Kingston Rovers have a [[Rugby league dual registration|dual registration]] agreement for the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Hughes |first1=Steven |title=Hull KR announce dual registration partners |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-announce-dual-registration-partners/ |access-date=7 March 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=19 November 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=O'Brien |first1=James |title='I'd love to play' - Milnes discusses Hull KR chances after Dewsbury outing |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rowan-milnes-hull-kr-dewsbury-6762662 |access-date=7 March 2022 |work=HullLive |date=7 March 2022}}</ref>

==== Rochdale Hornets (loan) ====
Laulu-Togaga'e also had a period on [[Loan (sports)|loan]] at [[Rochdale Hornets]] in 2022.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Hull KR youngster Phoenix Laulu-Togaga'e heads to League 1 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-youngster-phoenix-laulu-togagae-heads-to-league-1/ |access-date=24 July 2022 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=13 May 2022}}</ref>

==== Oldham R.L.F.C. (loan) ====
On 15 March 2024, he signed for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] in [[RFL League One|League One]] on season-long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 March 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Hull KR starlet Phoenix Laulu-Togaga’e makes season-long loan move to League 1|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-starlet-phoenix-laulu-togagae-loan-oldham-rlfc-league-1}}</ref>

On 17 October 2024, the loan period was extended, with him remaining at [[Oldham RLFC]] for the duration of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=The Oldham Times|date=17 Oct 2024|author=Kasey Smith|title=Phoenix set to continue his rise at Oldham next year|url=https://www.theoldhamtimes.co.uk/sport/24654290.phoenix-set-continue-rise-oldham-next-year/}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
On 24 October 2025, he signed for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-year deal for the 2026 season.<ref>{{cite news |last1=McAllister |first1=Josh |title=Hull KR fresh exit confirmed as full-back makes Super League switch |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/hull-kr-fresh-exit-confirmed-1464233 |access-date=27 April 2026 |work=All Out Rugby League |date=24 October 2025 |language=en}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 27 April 2026, it was confirmed that Laulu-Togaga'e had signed a three-and-a-half-year deal to play for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Apr 2026|accessdate=27 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Castleford Tigers land fullback in immediate transfer from Super League rivals|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-land-fullback-in-immediate-transfer-from-super-league-rivals}}</ref>
